successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts exists and exports LeaveType with values ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY; LeaveRequestStatus with values PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED; and LeaveRequest containing id, employeeId, leaveType, and status fields.

src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exists and exports LeaveRequestRepository with methods create(request: LeaveRequest): Promise<void>, findById(id: string): Promise<LeaveRequest | null>, and updateStatus(id: string, status: LeaveRequestStatus): Promise<void>.
src/modules/leave/leave.repository.ts exports PgLeaveRequestRepository implementing the LeaveRequestRepository contract as a PostgreSQL-facing repository type.
tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.repository.contract.spec.ts exists and validates LeaveRequest type usage and repository contract compatibility using Vitest.
All imports between leave.model.ts, leave.repository.ts, and the unit test resolve successfully and compile under TypeScript.
Project architecture documentation is updated to record LeaveType values (ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY) and LeaveRequestStatus values (PENDING, APPROVED, REJECTED).

constraints: Limit scope to type definitions, repository contracts, and associated unit tests only; do not implement business logic or persistence behavior.
Maintain modular-monolith boundaries by introducing artifacts only within the leave module and related tests/documentation.
Repository pattern: all database access must be represented through repository interfaces; no direct database access in domain types.
Repository contracts must operate on LeaveRequest types defined in src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts.
Use TypeScript compatible with Node.js 20 and project tooling.
Include Vitest-based unit tests for model and repository contract validation.
Audit records are required for state-changing operations project-wide, but no state-changing implementation is in scope for this phase.
Input validation at API boundaries is a project principle, but no API endpoints are introduced in this phase.
RBAC enforcement is a project principle, but no API endpoints are introduced in this phase.
No sensitive data in logs; no logging should be introduced in this phase.
Error handling principle applies to implementations, but no async operational logic should be added in this phase.
outOfScope: Leave approval or rejection workflow services.
API routes, controllers, request validation, or RBAC enforcement.
Database schema migrations or SQL execution logic.
Repository method implementations beyond contract/type definitions.
Employee domain model or repository creation.
LeaveBalance domain model, balance deductions, or balance services.
LeavePolicy domain model or policy logic.
Notification domain model, repositories, or notification processing.
AuditRecord domain model or repository contracts.
Workflow orchestration and lifecycle behavior beyond defining status types.
Any files outside the leave module, unit tests, and required architecture documentation updates for this phase.

ambiguities.description: The intent references defining LeaveRequest and submission DTO/types, but the detailed architecture only specifies LeaveRequest, LeaveType, and LeaveRequestStatus.
options: Create only LeaveRequest, LeaveType, and LeaveRequestStatus as defined in the architecture specification.
Add an additional submission DTO type alongside the specified LeaveRequest model.

ambiguities.description: The PostgreSQL-facing repository contract is specified as a class with method signatures only, but implementation depth is not explicitly stated.
options: Provide only class and method declarations/types without persistence logic.
Add stub method bodies that satisfy TypeScript compilation but perform no database operations.
